The exact classification of Afrohybanthus travancoricus is still being researched and may be subject to revision. The classification is based on various taxonomic characteristics, including morphological and molecular traits.

intro: Afrohybanthus travancoricus, also known as the Travancore Afrohybanthus, is a plant species belonging to the Violaceae family, commonly found in the tropical regions of India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Afrohybanthus travancoricus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of a powder or an ointment to treat various health conditions.
